title: Review Cadence Findings
prompt: Given a plugin you have not seen before, outline the review passes you would run and the first five findings categories you would report. Lead with risk, not style.
hints: A good review has ordered passes and findings tied to behavior, security, data, performance, and release risk.

model_answer.summary: Start with the plugin contract and then review the areas most likely to cause user-facing risk, regression, vulnerability, data loss, or release failure.
model_answer.work: Run ordered passes for contract, security, data, performance, tests, and release readiness. First findings should not style-nit the code; they should report material behavior risk with severity, file, line, reproduction steps, impact, and evidence. Categories to lead with are permission and nonce failures, unsafe validate/sanitize/escape paths, migration or deletion data loss, frontend or query performance regressions, missing tests around risky behavior, and release metadata or privacy gaps. Style comments can wait unless they hide a behavior problem.
checks: Confirm the review starts with contract and user-facing behavior.
For each finding, include severity, file, line, reproduction, impact, and evidence.
Prioritize security, data, performance, tests, and release risks before style.
Verify at least one finding category maps to a real behavior or regression risk.
Ask whether the report would help an engineer fix the issue without guessing.
model_answer.why_this_is_good: It teaches review as risk communication, which is much more valuable than a pile of preferences.
common_misses: Starting with formatting instead of behavior.
Reporting findings without file and line references.
Naming a risk without reproduction or impact.
model_answer.revision_prompt: Revise by ordering the review passes and writing the first finding categories with severity, file, line, reproduction, impact, evidence, and material risk.
